title: The Great Expedition of the Sas-ma-ras-ma-fla-mas Frog
summary: Mickey, Minnie, and Mole borrow a magical boat from the Princess of Mouseland and embark on an adventure to a remote island with a towering mountain. After several comedic false starts that require sailing home for water and Wheat Thins, the trio finally prepares a massive picnic and returns to the island. They trek through a dense jungle filled with butterflies and follow Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza to a fresh stream. There, they meet a giant, colorful bird named Lasra-bird who flies them to the mountain's peak. On the summit, they encounter a rare Sas-ma-ras-ma-fla-mas frog and discover a mysterious mushroom that smells like bacon. Following a successful picnic and a nap, the companions fly back to their boat and sail home just in time for bed.

highlightQuote.text: Next time when you get another crab in your shoe, you must call the Super Sleuths and they will come and help you when you have a crab in your shoe.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What are those for?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Turn off the lamps, please.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yep. Let me just get the stars all set up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (singing/vocalizing)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Now we're ready.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want the story about Mickey and Minnie and Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Don't look.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want the story about Mickey and Minnie and Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Well, let's see. Last time when we were telling a story about Mickey and Minnie and Mole, they'd just gone on a great adventure across the ocean in their new boat that they had borrowed from the Princess of Mouseland. And this beautiful magical boat had taken them all the way across the ocean to a little island that had on it a big mountain. And so, they brought the ocean, the boat right up to the shore and they jumped out into the shallow water. Kersplash, kersplash, kersplash! That was Mickey and Minnie and Mole all jumping in. And they waded up onto the beach and felt the nice yellow warm sand in their toes and they looked all around.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey, look, Minnie and Mickey, it's a light shining to you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they saw a light shining...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's me. I'm shining the light now. That's me. That's a flashlight, Mickey and Minnie and Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mole said, "Aha! There's someone on this island."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I'm not on that island.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're not?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Where are you?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm in my room and I'm shining the light.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see, said Mole. Would you like to come with us on our adventure to the island?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. What would you like to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to listen to the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So keep, and continue.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll continue. It's easiest for Daddy to tell the story if I don't have the flashlight shining in my eyes, so please don't shine it directly on my face. You could shine it anywhere else that you'd like. Thank you. Now then, Mickey and Minnie and Mole were walking up the beach and did they decide to dig in the sand or go up to the beach into the jungle or to look out to the ocean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or I could put the flashlight pointing down onto my knee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what do you notice when that... when you do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or I could turn it off. That's true too. (Click) I'll turn the flashlight off.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now tell the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now which of those things do you think that they did? Or something else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Minnie said, "Gosh, that was a long journey. I'm glad we made it to this island." And Mickey said, "Yeah, and you know what? I'm... I'm feeling a little thirsty." And Minnie said, "Hmm, me too. We'd better find some water." And Mole said, "Well, we can't drink the ocean water. That's too salty."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You must go home and make you a fresh drink.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A fresh drink would be nice, said Minnie, but...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, yeah, you could just sail home. Sail, sail, sail.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So they sailed back home really fast and got some fresh drinks (Slurping sounds). Yum! And then sailed back to the island to continue their adventure. And so then they got back off the boat onto the beach again. And then Mickey said, "Well, I'm glad we went back home for a drink. I'm not thirsty anymore. But you know what? I'm a little hungry." And Mole said, "Oh, no, we're not going all the way back home again just for a snack." But Minnie said...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, maybe we can search in the jungle to find something to eat. What do you think about that? No, there's nothing in the jungle to eat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There isn't?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, you just sail right back home and just have some time there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We should just sail back home again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mm-hm. And just have some time there singing, doing your thing home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, singing? Okay. Well, let's just go home and do our thing and have a snack there, said Minnie. And Mickey said, "Well, all right." And Mole said, "Oh my gosh, again? Okay. Can I come over to your place and have a snack?" And Mickey and Minnie said, "Sure." And so they sailed (Sailing sounds) all the way back home and they got out and went to Mickey and Minnie's house where they had a snack and then they sang a song. What did they sing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (singing) Um, ad-zo-willo and adonna palm on the sun. And the beach is going on sun and those are waves. In the sea of willow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. What was that song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was about the beach.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Is there more of it that you could sing? I'd like to hear more.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, there's not anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's the whole song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's just a little bit more. (singing) On its other bill and stornieso and a whale in car-da-bee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Was there a whale in there somewhere?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what else was in that song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A carnabee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's a carnabee?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a kind of shark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A carnabee shark? Wow. Is that a friendly kind of shark or a scary one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A friendly, kind of mean, good, slow, kind of luxury, chompy, kind shark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yeah, chompy shark. Well, Mickey and Minnie thought that Mole's song was very beautiful and they said, "Gosh, we've had a big adventure already going back and forth to our island. Should we go back again because you know, I had an idea," said Mickey.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Minnie said, "What was your idea?" And Mickey said, "Well, it's for an adventure that might be a challenging adventure, but I think we could do it." And Mole said, "What is it? What is it? What is it?" And Mickey said, "Here's what I think. I think if we go back to that island, we could try to climb the mountain and see what's all the way on top." And Mole said, "I don't know if we can do it. That seems like a very tall mountain." But Minnie said, "I don't know, we could do it I think." And Mickey said, "Yeah, but you know, I learned a very important lesson today, which is this: if we're going to climb a mountain, we'd better bring snacks and drinks." And Mole said, "Yeah, provisions." And so they got out their biggest picnic basket and their backpacks and they filled them up with all kinds of provisions. What did they bring, do you think?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're chewing on your hair.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They bring corn, Wheat Thins, apples, plums, bananas, crackers, Goldfish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And also Mole, who really likes to eat worms, brought a bag of wriggly, wiggly worms. And they brought some drinks, some water, because each of them had their own separate water bottle, because that's very important for hiking up a mountain. And they brought bug spray and hats and hiking boots and a map of the island. And then they said to each other, "I think we're ready," Mickey said. And Minnie said, "Yep, we've got everything." And Mole said, "Oh my gosh, can we do it? I'm so excited!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they hopped in their boat and they sailed once again all the way across the big blue ocean, riding on the waves, with the water splashing all around them and the sun beating down and beautiful white seagulls flapping around, going (Seagull sounds).

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Gerdon, Gerdon-a-rat-to-three.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, and the birds said all those things. And also the birds flew down and tried to eat their snacks, but no one would let them because they needed their snacks for their hike. And they sailed their boat all the way to the island and pulled it up onto the beach and hopped out onto the sand and said, "Okay," Mickey said, "let's go!" And he set off walking down the beach and Minnie said, "Wait a minute, where are you going?" And Mickey said, "I'm on our hike." But Minnie said, "No, we're going up the mountain, it's that way!" and she pointed straight up the mountain. And Mickey said, "Haha, oh yeah, you're right." And Mole said, "Yeah, we'd better go up the mountain. Let's follow the map." And so they started their hike. And the first part of their hike was just to get up to the top of the beach. And so they walked through the sand. Tromp, tromp, tromp, tromp, tromp. And then all of a sudden, Mickey said, "Wait! I have a problem!" and he picked up his foot and looked at his shoe. And Minnie said, "Well, what's the matter?" And Mickey said, "I think I have some sand in my shoe. I'd better shake it out." And so he took off his shoe...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, you just dump it out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mickey said, "Oh yeah, I'll just dump it out. (Sound effects)." And so much sand got out... came out of his shoe and went (Sound effects) and then plop, plop, plop, three little seashells came out. And then thump, a tiny little crab came out and scuttled away. And Mickey said, "Oh my gosh, I had a crab in my shoe! That's ridiculous!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was... that was the problem. Next time when you get another crab in your shoe, you must call the Super Sleuths and they will come and help you when you have a crab in your shoe.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mickey said, "That's a good idea. Next time I'll call the Super Sleuths. But now, we have to continue on our adventure up to the top of the mountain," and he pointed way up high to the top of the mountain. "But before we can go there, we have to cross through this, what's right in front of us: the jungle." Now, the jungle is a very dense kind of forest that grows where it's hot and wet because there's lots of rain. And in those places, there are lots of different kinds of plants and animals and bugs and creepy crawlies and birds and vines and mushrooms, all kinds of things that grow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can... hey little mice, I figured out that there's mushrooms in the jungle and you can try those mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the Mole said, "Yeah, you know, that's a good idea."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, what do you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I see my curtain and I think I see some plants.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yeah, let's go into the jungle, said Mole, and maybe we can find some mushrooms to snack on.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And maybe, uh, I see... and Mole, I think I see some wiggly spriggly worms that you can have.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Those are my favorite," said Mole. "I love those even more than mushrooms." And so they tromped into the jungle and they walked into the deep dark jungle that was full of big, big trees and dense, dense bushes and plants and vines. And there were little tiny ants crawling all over the place and beautiful butterflies fluttering around that were red and green and blue and purple. And there were little tiny frogs hopping all over the place. And there were birds flapping through the tops of the trees.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, I think I... I made the frogs hop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the frogs were hopping around in the jungle because they love to hop around in the jungle. And Minnie said, "Oh, I see those frogs hopping around. I wonder where they're hopping to. Maybe we should follow one." And so they found a tiny little frog. And what color was the frog?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Green, because all of the frogs are green and red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm. And so they followed the green and red frog. It was a tiny, tiny frog and its name was... oh, what was its name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza, a tiny, tiny frog was hopping through the jungle. And they followed Cindy-Wan until he jumped, splash, right into a little stream. And Minnie said, "Aha! I thought we might find some water this way. I know frogs like water. And since we followed Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza all this way, we made it to this pretty little stream and now we can drink some fresh water." And Mickey said (Drinking sound).

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yum! (Slurping sounds).

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "It's delicious! So refreshing!" And... and the Mole said, "Yeah, I've been getting a little bit hot from all this hiking. I'm going to splash some water on my face (Splashing sounds)." And while they were doing that, they heard the sound of another animal. What was that animal?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a (Bird calling sounds). Who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a huge, beautiful tropical bird that had feathers in red and green and gold and purple.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that huge tropical colorful bird flapped right down to the other side of the stream and looked over at our adventurers and opened her giant beak and said, "Oh, hey! Who are you?" And Mickey and Minnie and Mole said, "Oh, we're just on an adventure. I'm Mickey, I'm Minnie, and I'm the Mole." And the bird said, "Well, my name is..."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Kees-a-las-tra-dro-na-ras-ba bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Keesalastradronarasba bird! "But you can call me Lasra-bird for short." And Mole said, "Oh, nice to meet you, Lasra-bird. We're trying to climb all the way to the top of the mountain." And Lasra-bird said, "Well, that sounds... whew, that's a long way! I... I fly there all the time, but for you little creatures, that might be a long hike. Do you think you could do it?" And Minnie said, "Well, we packed a very nice lunch." And Mickey said, "You know, you're a big bird." And Lasra-bird said, "Huh. And you're small creatures. Maybe you could climb on my back! But since you mentioned you have a nice lunch, maybe you'd give me a snack first because I'm a little hungry." And what did Mickey and Minnie and Mole give to the bird to have a snack? Did they offer anything that they had brought, like some corn maybe or some of the worms or some...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some of... the bird likes ants.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, yes!" The Mole was like, "Hmm, I happen to know that this type of bird likes ants. We didn't bring any, but we could find some." And how did they find the ants?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They got a bag and they packed the ants in with the worms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, so they got some ants and they offered it to Lasra-bird who said, "Oh, thank you (Eating sounds). Oh, excuse me, I just love those ants so much, they're so delicious! Okay, let's fly to the top of the mountain!" And so then Mickey and Minnie and the Mole climbed onto Lasra-bird's back and gripped tightly onto the red and purple and gold feathers on the bird's back. And the bird said, "Okay, everyone, are you ready to fly?" And Mole said, "I don't know, I think... I'm not sure, I hope... I hope it's going to be okay, I'm a little nervous." And Lasra-bird said, "Don't worry, I'm a very good flyer."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And suddenly there was a huge (Whoosh) as Lasra-bird's wings opened wide and she jumped up into the air and flapped her wings again. And suddenly she was rising higher and higher above the jungle. And the tiny mice and the Mole looked down and they saw their boat floating in the water at the beach. And they saw the sandy beach and they saw the jungle they'd just been in. And then the bird flew around in a big circle and they saw in front of them the huge mountain. And then Lasra-bird went flap, flap, flap and flew them higher and higher up the mountain. And what did they do while they were riding on the bird? Did they sing a little flying song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so flap, flap, flap, flap, the bird took them higher and higher and higher, past so many trees and rocks and streams and waterfalls on the side of the mountain and up through little fluffy clouds and higher and higher up into the sky. And it started to get a little bit colder and now the mountain was nothing but rocks. And they made it all the way to the tippy-tippy-top, the pointy top of the mountain. And Lasra-bird landed gently, boop, right on top, and lowered her wings so that Mickey and Minnie and Mole could slide right down and stand on the top of the mountain. And what did they do next? Did they look around, or what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They looked... they looked around.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did they see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A sas-ma-ras-ma-fla-mas-frog-in-dine frog.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's a kind of frog?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's a special kind of frog that lives on the top of mountains. It's a very rare frog and almost no one ever gets to meet them because you can... you have to go to the top of a mountain to find one. And what did this frog have to say for itself?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ribbit!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: (Laughs). And what else did they see on the top of the mountain?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Bird sounds)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: (Bird sounds).

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seagull!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A seagull came flying by and said, "Hey everybody, I'm hungry, do you have anything for lunch?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and the seagull likes fishies.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, seagulls do love fishies. And Minnie said, "Well, we don't have any fish, but we have... we have some chips." And Seagull said, "I'll take one!" and went crunch, crunch, crunch with his beak and then flew away. And then they looked around some more and what did they see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the top of the mountain had a special kind of mushroom that only grows on tops of mountains. And what color was that mushroom?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Red... no, orange with yellow polka dots... no, an orange mushroom with yellow stripes down to the frost at the bottom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. And Mole said, "This is amazing! I've never seen a mushroom like this, but I've read about it in books. And so I know that it's safe to eat." And Mickey and Minnie said, "Are you sure, Mole?" And Mole said, "Um, I think so. Well, maybe we should just pick it and take it home and then we can dry it and decide to cook it later if we feel like it." And Minnie said, "Hmm, let me sniff it." And she sniffed it. "Mmm, it smells delicious!" What did it smell like? What flavor was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the stor... (sneeze) Achoo! Because it... it smelled like bacon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: (Laughs). And then they were all tired out from exploring the top of the mountain and they felt a little hungry. And so they sat down on the little blanket they had brought and they laid out their beautiful picnic. And the giant tropical bird that had taken them up there said, "Okay, I'll be back in a little bit to take you down. Bye!" and flew off, flap, flap, flap. And so they were by themselves on the top of the mountain and they had their little picnic and (Eating sounds) they ate everything all up. And then they felt very sleepy, so they thought, "Hmm, maybe we should take a quick nap here on top of the mountain." Did they take a nap?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, they took a nap and then... (Click/Noise). They woke up! They made a big giant poopy!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they did?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, they made one in their potty suits.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good thing they brought their potties because they had to poop. And then...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Mole peed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: (Laughs). And also Mole peed, yes, that's true. And then Mickey and Minnie and Mole thought to themselves, "Gosh, it's getting late. I hope that bird comes back pretty soon to take us home." And just at that moment, they saw a little speck way high up in the sky. And it got a little bit bigger and a little bit bigger and they saw wings flapping, flap, flap, flap. And they saw red and gold and purple feathers. And they saw it was their friend the bird come to take them home. And the bird landed on the top of the mountain and they climbed on his back. And he flew them down, down, down (Whoosh) all the way down the mountain and over the jungle and over the beach to their boat. And he dropped them off and they sailed home and then they climbed in their beds to go to sleep.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The end.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's turn off the stars and do space-pop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a good idea.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Princess of Mouseland
characters.description: Adorned in a shimmering, star-dusted gown and a crown of deep sapphire, this royal mouse governs her miniature kingdom by bestowing ceremonial names upon every wheel of artisanal cheese. Beneath her whimsical duties lies a legacy of enchantment, as she is the sovereign owner of the magical vessel currently ferrying the adventurers across the realms.

characters.name: Carnabee Shark
characters.description: A unique type of shark described as friendly, mean, slow, luxury, and chompy.

characters.name: Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza
characters.description: A tiny green and red frog that lives in the jungle and leads the way to water.

characters.name: Keesalastradronarasba bird
characters.description: Also known as Lasra-bird; a giant, colorful tropical bird who loves eating ants and gives mountain rides.

characters.name: Sas-ma-ras-ma-fla-mas-frog-in-dine frog
characters.description: A rare species of frog that lives exclusively at the tippy-tippy-top of mountains.

places.name: Mouseland
places.description: The ancestral homeland of Mickey, Minnie, and the Princess, this storied kingdom serves as both the royal residence and the vital port where the group ventures to reclaim their boat.

places.name: The Island
places.description: Across the vast ocean lies a remote expanse where sun-bleached sandy beaches give way to a suffocatingly dense jungle. Above the emerald canopy, a range of jagged, cloud-piercing mountains dominates the horizon, shielding a lonely playground nestled strangely within the wild, untamed terrain.

places.name: The Big Mountain
places.description: A tall, rocky peak that is the ultimate destination of the hike.

places.name: The Jungle
places.description: Dimitar’s sanctuary is a sweltering, vapor-thick forest of towering trees and emerald vines, where he builds his home beneath a heavy canopy that shelters kaleidoscopic butterflies and the enigmatic Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza. This humid wilderness, teeming with chattering monkeys and a roaming elephant, serves as a wild transit for racers who must navigate its dense, wet depths twice.

places.name: Mickey
places.description: The place where the group returns multiple times to gather snacks and sing songs.

places.name: Minnie's house
places.description: The place where the group returns multiple times to gather snacks and sing songs.

places.name: The Stream
places.description: Tucked within the lush jungle near the mouth of Goofy’s tunnel, this refreshing basin offers a cool reprieve to any traveler patient enough to follow a local frog's lead. Its mirror-still surface serves as a somber sanctuary where Eeyore retreats on his birthday, spending the hours in quiet contemplation of his own reflection.
